Fern Porrucel PnecrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street e Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5ffi . Fax (916) 322-0886
Novernber 30,2W
The Honorable Steve Messina
City ofBenicia
250 East L Street
Benicia, CA 94510
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-04-200
Dear Mr- Messina:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ..Act ).,
QUESTTON
Does the employment of an attorneyby a corporation in which you have a
substantial financial interest create a conflict of interest between you and the attomey,s
other clients?
CONCLUSION
No- Since the attorney is not a source of income to you, the attorney,s other
clients are not sources of income to you by virtue of this relationship alone.-
FACTS
You are the directly elected mayor of the City of Benicia and you have a
substantial financial interest in227 First Street,LLC. 221 First Streei, LLC intends to
construct a nine-unit commerciayresidential project in Benicia and has engaged the
services of Dana Dean, Esq. to represent the limited liability company in a development
application with the City ofBenicia. Ms. Dean has several "ti*tr *to have applications
and other matters pending before the city.
lGovemmentCode sections8l000-91014. CommissionregulationsappearatTitle2,sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
is,!5


File No. A-04-200
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused Uyitreir own financial interests.
($ 81001, subd. (b).) Specifically, section 87100 prohibits anypublic official from
"making" "participating in making" or othenrise using his or her official position to
'tnfluence" a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. The
Commission has adopted a standard eight-step analysis fordeciding whether an official
has a disqualifting conflict of interest. (Reg. 19700, suM. @[l) - isl.l Because your
question implicates gnly one particular step, step three, we proceed diiectly in the
analysis to that step.'
Step Three- Sources of Income
The Act's oonflict-oGinterest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from economic interests. These economic interests are descn-bed at regulations 18703.1
through I 87 03.5, inclusive.
o A public official has an economic interest in a business eNrtity in which he or she has a
direct or indirect investnent of $2,000 ormore (g g7103(a); reg. ls703.l(a)); or in
which he or she is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position
of management ($ 87103(d); reg. 18703.1(b));
o A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a
direct or indirect interest of $2,000 ormore ($ 97103(b);-reg- $7a3.2);
o A public official has an economic interest in any source of incomg including
promised income, which aggregates to $500 ormore within 12 months prior to the
decision (g 87103(c);reg. $TA33);
o A public official has an economic interest in any souroe ofgifts to him or her if the
gifts aggregate to $340 or more within l2 months prior to the decision (g 87103(e);
rcg.187A3.4);
o
A public official has an economic interest in his orherpersonal finances, including
those ofhis orherimmediate fanily - this is the'lersonal financial effects,'nrle.
($ 87103; reg. 18703.5.)
Your question asks whether you will have a conflict of interest with Ms. Dean's
other clients solely by virtue of your company's hiring ofMs. Dean. Applying the terms
of section 87103 and regulations 18703.1 - 18703.5, howev€,r, we see tfrat trrts. Dean,
though your attorney, is not a source of income to you.
' St"p* one and two, whether you are a public official and whether you are participating in
governmental decisions, are presurned met in the circumstances you describe


FileNo. A-04-200
Page No. 3
Because Ms- Dean is not a source of income to you, impacts on herbusiness caused
by your decisions affecting the clients ofherbusiness need not be considered . (See e.g.,
regulation 1 8705.1 .) In addition, her clients me not, by virtue of this relationship alon-e,-
sources of income to you. As a result, you do not have a conflict of interest with respect
to other clients of Ms. Dean should they have matters before the city in the future. Of
course, if Ms- Dean's clients are independently economic interests of yours as described
above (section 87103), you mayhave a conflict of inte,rest when Ms. Dean appears before
you on theirbehalf. You haveprovided no facts forus to anallze this further-
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely'
Luisa
General
J
By: C. Soott
Senior
Ineal Division
